description: The longest ancient beam-style stone bridge in China, renowned as 'No bridge under heaven is as long as this bridge,' a marvel of ancient engineering.
An important worship site for Mazu belief along China's southeast coast, featuring ancient architecture and thriving incense offerings.
Ruins of ancient city gates from the Song and Yuan dynasties, bearing witness to the historical development of Quanzhou's ancient city, with high archaeological value.
Ruins of the southern gate of the ancient city from the Song and Yuan dynasties, reflecting the historical changes of Quanzhou.
Stretching coastline equipped with seaside boardwalks, a premium coastal leisure destination near Quanzhou.
The largest comprehensive park in Quanzhou city, featuring picturesque lakes and mountains, ideal for leisurely strolls.
A quintessential example of Minnan red-brick mansions, a masterpiece of overseas Chinese architectural art from the Qing Dynasty.
Showcasing the historical and cultural ties between Fujian and Taiwan, providing insights into the development of cross-strait exchanges.
Quanzhou's most historically rich ancient street, preserving well-maintained Minnan architectural styles, and a hub for local delicacies and snacks.
